intro: Lampranthus rustii, also known as the Red Carpet, is a low-growing, succulent plant species native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lampranthus rustii include flavonoids, glycosides, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its medicinal and therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Lampranthus rustii include capsules, tablets, and teas, often used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
